---
title: "🔷 Glossary"
body-classes: no-chapter-nav
format:
  html:
    toc: false
---

```{r}
#| label: glossary-table
#| echo: false

glossary <- read.csv2(
  here::here("files/data", "glossary.csv")
) |>
  janitor::clean_names() |>
  dplyr::rename(
    r_function = r_function,
    definition = definition
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    r_function = stringr::str_trim(r_function),
    definition = stringr::str_squish(definition)
  ) |>
  dplyr::arrange(r_function)

glossary |>
  gt::gt() |>
  gt::cols_label(
    r_function = "R Function",
    definition = "Definition"
  ) |>
  gt::cols_width(
    r_function ~ gt::px(170),
    definition ~ gt::pct(80)
  ) |>
  gt::cols_align(
    align = "left",
    columns = gt::everything()
  ) |>
  # Header styling
  gt::tab_style(
    style = gt::cell_text(
      weight = "bold",
      color = "#539FC6"
    ),
    locations = gt::cells_column_labels()
  ) |>
  gt::tab_style(
    style = gt::cell_borders(
      sides = "bottom",
      color = "#539FC6",
      weight = gt::px(2)
    ),
    locations = gt::cells_column_labels()
  ) |>
  # Code-style function column
  gt::tab_style(
    style = list(
      gt::cell_text(
        font = "monospace",
        size = gt::px(14)
      ),
      gt::cell_fill(color = "white")
    ),
    locations = gt::cells_body(columns = r_function)
  ) |>
  # Table layout (textbook style)
  gt::tab_options(
    table.align = "left",
    table.width = gt::pct(100),
    data_row.padding = gt::px(8),
    column_labels.padding = gt::px(10),
    table.border.top.style = "none",
    table.border.bottom.style = "none",
    table_body.border.top.style = "solid",
    table_body.border.top.width = gt::px(1),
    table_body.border.top.color = "#e6e6e6",
    table_body.hlines.style = "solid",
    table_body.hlines.width = gt::px(1),
    table_body.hlines.color = "#eeeeee",
    row.striping.include_table_body = TRUE
  ) |>
  gt::opt_row_striping()
```
